title: Where to Stay in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Choose the Kiez by trip friction, not fantasy.** First-timers usually do best in Mitte or around Hackescher Markt; nightlife-led visitors should look at Kreuzberg or Friedrichshain.
**Families and food-led stays work better in calmer bases** such as Prenzlauer Berg, Schöneberg or Charlottenburg, where mornings and late returns feel easier.
**Budget stays can work in Friedrichshain or northern Neukölln**, but only if you are comfortable using the U-Bahn/S-Bahn and do not need every sight on your doorstep.
**Transport matters more than the neighborhood name.** A hotel beside the right U-Bahn/S-Bahn line beats a famous Kiez with awkward connections.
**Book early for summer and December.** Berlin can look relaxed, but good-value rooms disappear quickly in peak months.
